title: microlepia thwaitesii
intro: Microlepia thwaitesii is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae, commonly found in the tropical regions of Asia, particularly in Sri Lanka. This plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds that can help alleviate pain and reduce inflammation. The health benefits of Microlepia thwaitesii include its potential to treat various conditions such as arthritis, rheumatism, and skin diseases, as well as its ability to boost the immune system and act as an antiseptic. Microlepia thwaitesii is often prepared as an herbal tea, decoction, or powder, and can also be applied topically as a paste or salve to treat external wounds and skin conditions.
